name: Ticarcillin/clavulanic acid
executiveSummary: Historical antipseudomonal in combination; use according to antibiogram and institutional availability.
indications:
## Indications

- Gram-negative infections, including *Pseudomonas*, in combination regimens.
- Alternative according to local susceptibility when included in the formulary.

## Precautions

- Penicillin allergy.
- Adjust for chronic kidney disease (CKD).
- Use restricted due to the availability of alternatives.

> Adjust according to institutional protocols and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: 3.1 g vial (3 g ticarcillin + 0.1 g clavulanate).
adulto.reconstitution: Reconstitute with 13 mL of diluent according to package insert.

pediatrico.dose: 200–400 mg/kg/day of ticarcillin divided every 4–6 h (max. per protocol).

stability:
## Stability

- Use dilution within 6–24 h depending on diluent and package insert.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

### Frequent

- Diarrhea, phlebitis, hypokalemia with high doses.

### Serious

- Anaphylaxis, *C. difficile* colitis.

bibliography.citation: ANMAT. Drug information and authorized prescribing information in Argentina.
Argentine Society of Infectious Diseases (SADI). Guidelines and consensus statements.
Infectious Diseases Society of America (IDSA). Clinical practice guidelines.
